predictor: SIRS-positive status (fulfilling at least two SIRS criteria)
standardized_predictor: SIRS-positive status (fulfilling at least two SIRS criteria)
performance: SIRS assessment missed approximately 1 in 6 patients with infections at high risk of death
notes: SIRS-negative status defined as fulfilling zero or one SIRS criterion. Study conducted in ICUs of a developing country. Comparison made against Sepsis-3 (SOFA-based) definition.

performance: AUROC lower than SOFA; lower than Kaukonen's study
notes: APACHE II scores ≥24 in 55.6% of patients. SIRS AUROC was much lower than SOFA AUROC for discriminating hospital mortality.
SOFA demonstrated better discrimination of hospital mortality compared to SIRS.

population: ICU patients with sepsis or septic shock during initial episode of suspected infection
predictor: SIRS criteria (abnormal temperature, increased heart rate, increased respiratory rate or decreased PaCO2, abnormal white-cell count)
standardized_predictor: SIRS criteria (abnormal temperature, increased heart rate, increased respiratory rate or decreased PaCO2, abnormal white-cell count)
outcome: Diagnosis of sepsis (SIRS-positive vs SIRS-negative severe sepsis)
standardized_outcome: Diagnosis of sepsis (SIRS-positive vs SIRS-negative severe sepsis)
timing: Initial episode of suspected infection during ICU stay
effect_size: SIRS-positive: 538/631 (85.3%); SIRS-negative: 93/631 (14.7%); Median SIRS criteria met: 3 (IQR 2-3) in SIRS-positive vs 1 (IQR 1-1) in SIRS-negative
performance: P < 0.001 for all comparisons between SIRS-positive and SIRS-negative groups
notes: Abnormal temperature met in 46.5% of SIRS-positive vs 6.5% of SIRS-negative; Increased heart rate met in 94.1% vs 46.2%; Increased respiratory rate or decreased PaCO2 met in 83.3% vs 16.1%; Abnormal white-cell count met in 61.6% vs 14.1%. Distribution of SIRS criteria count: 0 criteria in 2.5%, 1 criterion in 12.2%, 2 criteria in 31.7%, 3 criteria in 34.5%, 4 criteria in 19.0% of all patients.
